title: poa araratica subsp. oligophylla
intro: Poa araratica subsp. oligophylla, also known as the few-leaved meadow-grass, is a subspecies of the Poa araratica plant, a type of grass that is native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Poa araratica subsp. oligophylla include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
